vulnerabilities.title: Broken Access Control (IDOR) on Permintaan Kegiatan detail/update actions
vulnerabilities.description: Several controller actions fetch `PermintaanKegiatan` by ID but do not enforce ownership/role checks consistently, enabling authenticated users to access or modify other users' records.
vulnerabilities.poc: Login as regular user A, then request `/admin/permintaan-kegiatan/{id}` with an ID owned by user B (or submit PATCH/POST to update/proses endpoints). Because no ownership/role check is present in those actions, unauthorized data disclosure/modification is possible.
remediation: Add explicit authorization checks in every mutating/read action (policy or Gate).
Use route-model binding + policy methods (`view`, `update`, `delete`, custom `processConsumption`).
Return 403 on unauthorized access; do not rely on UI-level filtering only.
vulnerabilities.secure_rewrite: public function show(PermintaanKegiatan $permintaan) { $this->authorize('view', $permintaan); return view('admin.permintaan.show', compact('permintaan')); }

vulnerabilities.title: Missing authorization guard on room status toggle endpoint
vulnerabilities.description: `RuanganController::toggle` modifies room active state without Gate/policy authorization check, while route is only protected by `auth` middleware.
vulnerabilities.poc: Any authenticated low-privilege user can send PATCH `/admin/ruangan/{id}/toggle` and activate/deactivate rooms, impacting booking operations.
remediation: Add `abort_if(Gate::denies('ruangan_edit'), 403);` or policy check in controller.
Add feature tests asserting non-privileged users receive 403.

vulnerabilities.title: Stored XSS risk in DataTables raw HTML rendering
vulnerabilities.description: User-controlled fields are concatenated directly into HTML for DataTables and marked as raw columns, bypassing Blade escaping.
vulnerabilities.poc: Submit `nama_kegiatan` as `` and open the listing page. The payload is rendered as HTML in the table.
remediation: Escape dynamic output with `e()` before string concatenation, or avoid raw HTML concatenation.
Only use `rawColumns` for trusted static markup.
Add payload-based XSS regression tests for DataTables responses.

vulnerabilities.title: Unauthenticated API endpoints expose operational and personal data
vulnerabilities.type: Sensitive Data Exposure / API Security Misconfiguration
vulnerabilities.description: Public API routes under `/api/v1/signage*` are unauthenticated and return internal data (requester name, participant count, driver/trip details, event metadata).

affected_code.snippet: getCars()/getPendingRequests() return driver names, tujuan, keperluan, pemohon, jumlah_peserta

vulnerabilities.owasp: A01:2021 Broken Access Control / A05:2021 Security Misconfiguration
vulnerabilities.poc: Anonymous users can call `/api/v1/signage/requests` or `/api/kiosk/events` directly and harvest scheduling/request data.
remediation: Require authentication (Sanctum/API key) for non-public data.
Minimize returned fields for public signage endpoints.
Apply rate limiting and monitoring for scraping abuse.

vulnerabilities.title: Mass-assignment overexposure in PermintaanKegiatan model
vulnerabilities.description: Model uses `protected $guarded = ['id'];`, allowing nearly all attributes to be mass-assigned if controller validation is bypassed or future code uses `$request->all()` with create/update.
vulnerabilities.poc: If a future endpoint directly updates model from request, attacker can inject sensitive fields (e.g., `status_permintaan`, `user_id`, approval flags).
remediation: Add tests to ensure sensitive columns cannot be client-controlled.

vulnerabilities.title: Session cookie secure flag not enforced by default
vulnerabilities.description: Session cookie `secure` depends on env and defaults to null, which may permit transmission over HTTP in misconfigured deployments.
vulnerabilities.poc: If deployed behind HTTP or missing env hardening, session cookies may be sent without `Secure` attribute and become interceptable on insecure transport.
remediation: Add deployment checks for cookie flags (`Secure`, `HttpOnly`, `SameSite`).

summary.notes: Primary risks are broken access control and XSS in admin workflows; API data exposure is also significant for privacy/operations.

recommendations: Adopt Laravel Policies for PermintaanKegiatan/Ruangan and enforce via route middleware.
Eliminate raw HTML concatenation or strictly escape user input before `rawColumns` rendering.
Harden public APIs: authentication, response minimization, and rate-limits.
Move to explicit `$fillable` model allowlists across domain models.
Create automated security regression tests (authorization + XSS payload tests).
Add secure deployment baseline: `APP_DEBUG=false`, `SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE=true`, HTTPS-only, and periodic secret rotation.
